responses.question: Could you briefly introduce yourself — your program, year of study, and how long you have been at HKUST(GZ)?
How would you describe the general social environment at HKUST(GZ)?
How would you describe your interactions with international students at HKUST(GZ)?
Do you think it is easy or difficult for Chinese and international students to become friends here? Why?
responses.answer: Easy, cause some internationals are lonely and willing to talk

responses.question: In your experience, do Chinese and international students mix naturally, or do they mostly stay in separate circles? Why?
responses.answer: Stay seperately, maybe people from the same country have more to talk about.

responses.question: What factors make interaction between Chinese and international students easier?
To what extent do language or cultural differences affect communication between Chinese and international students?
In your opinion, does social integration affect international students’ academic experiences? How?
Have you noticed any difference in your academic performance between UCUG and UFUG courses? Why?
From what you have observed, do international students seem to perform differently in UCUG and UFUG courses? Why?
Do you think international students sometimes face disadvantages in group work, class participation, or access to academic support because of weaker social connections? Why or why not?
responses.answer: No, in the contrary more advantages for them because of english environment

responses.question: Have you noticed any differences in how Chinese and international students participate in group projects, study groups, or classroom interaction?
Do you think students with stronger social networks generally perform better academically at HKUST(GZ)? Why?
responses.answer: No, it depends on work and talent.

responses.question: What do you think are the biggest barriers preventing stronger integration between Chinese and international students?
Do you think the university does enough to encourage interaction and inclusion between different student groups? Why or why not?
responses.answer: No, the university only cares about tuition fees

responses.question: Are there any university practices, campus habits, or classroom dynamics that unintentionally make integration harder?
What do you think HKUST(GZ) could do better to improve integration between Chinese and international students?
Is there anything about this issue that people often misunderstand or fail to notice?

summary_bullets: Second-year AI student with 1.5 years at HKUST(GZ).
Reports a lot of interaction with international students and says friendship is easy.
Believes students still stay in separate groups because same-country peers share more topics.
Thinks good English and personality help interaction; introversion and discrimination hinder it.
Sees language and culture differences as having a huge impact.
Says UCUG performance is lower because of an unclear grading system.
Does not think stronger social networks necessarily improve academic performance.
Attributes weak integration to the low percentage of international students and few campus activities.
